place: White Meadow Lake CDP, New Jersey, New Jersey

facts.text: White Meadow Lake’s name first appeared in 1774 when a 1,500‑acre survey was returned to the Board of Proprietors of Eastern New Jersey.
The Kline family turned the area into a lakeside community in the 1950s, marketing its proximity to New York City as a perfect weekend getaway.
In 1954, the Day Camp and its facilities were handed over to the Country Club and the White Meadow Lake Property Owners Association, sparking a boom in development.
The lake’s Olympic‑size pool, originally called the Marine Pool, was built to resemble an ocean liner’s deck and features a central diving tower.
White Meadow Lake sits largely within Sparta township, with a small portion extending into Byram Township.
The community’s alpine‑style architecture, with castle‑like buildings and a tiered boardwalk, was designed to give the area a distinctive mountain‑lake feel.
